hours_note: More than 1 in 4 worked more than 40 hours per week in 2024.
notes: Strong upside, but deadline cycles can bleed into evenings.

hours_note: Most work full time, and some work more than 40 hours per week.
notes: High upside with lower substitution risk than many generic analyst roles, but often tied to technical competence and ongoing learning.

hours_note: Most work full time.
notes: High-demand analytical path with strong upside and some AI resilience because it often involves building or governing AI systems.

hours_note: Most work full time, and some work more than 40 hours per week.
notes: Predictable office pattern for many roles, but a lot of task structure is AI-sensitive.

hours_note: Facilities that provide 24-hour care may require rotating shifts, weekends, and on-call time.
notes: Very durable and geographically flexible, but often costs nights, weekends, or holiday control.

hours_note: Most work full time, and some work more than 40 hours per week.
notes: Strong upside in healthcare without clinical training, but many roles want prior industry experience and organizational pressure can be high.

hours_note: Most work full time during the school year.
notes: Calendar rhythm is predictable, but prep and grading can spill beyond the school day.

hours_note: Most work full time, and longer hours are common at certain times of the year.
notes: Stable and structured, but repetitive knowledge work faces meaningful AI pressure.

hours_note: Most work full time, and some work more than 40 hours per week.
notes: Good upside, but higher-end finance paths often pull people toward major hubs and weaker boundaries.

hours_note: Most management analysts work full time and overtime is common.
notes: Strong earnings and mobility, but travel and client demands often eat into evenings.

hours_note: Most market research analysts work full time during regular business hours.
notes: Better hours than finance or consulting, but work is highly exposed to analytics automation and AI tooling.

hours_note: Most graphic designers work full time, but schedules can vary for self-employed workers.
notes: Creative lifestyle can feel flexible, but earnings are more modest and tooling is highly AI-exposed.

hours_note: Most web developers and digital designers work full time.
notes: Combines design and tech with good upside, but interface work is being reshaped quickly by AI tooling.

hours_note: Most public relations specialists work full time. Some work more than 40 hours per week.
notes: Good for communicators, but reputation work can mean urgency, client pressure, and more hub concentration than students expect.

hours_note: Work schedules may include evenings and weekends and may vary during bad weather.
notes: Strong local demand and low AI risk, with a clearer end-of-day cutoff than many office jobs.

hours_note: Most plumbers, pipefitters, and steamfitters work full time, including nights and weekends, and they are often on call to handle emergencies.
notes: Great paid-training path with local durability, but emergency work can disrupt evenings and weekends.

hours_note: Work may include evening or weekend hours, especially during peak repair seasons.
notes: Strong practical route with decent pay and low AI exposure, but schedule control depends on service demand.

hours_note: Most flooring installers and tile and stone setters work full time.
notes: Physically demanding, but often easier to protect evenings and weekends than in elite knowledge-work tracks.

hours_note: Most construction managers work full time, and some work more than 40 hours per week.
notes: High pay with strong real-world demand, but schedules move with projects and deadlines.

hours_note: Most physicians and surgeons work full time. Some worked more than 60 hours per week in 2024.
notes: Extremely strong earnings, but the lifestyle cost is often nights, weekends, and limited schedule control.

hours_note: Most work full time.
notes: Analytical and policy-oriented with strong boundaries for many roles, but fewer openings and more hub concentration than general business jobs.

hours_note: Most biological technicians work full time.
notes: Good science-adjacent entry path, but the earnings ceiling is lower unless you add more schooling.

hours_note: Most civil engineers work full time, and some work more than 40 hours per week.
notes: Strong infrastructure-linked career with durable demand, though project deadlines can stretch the workweek.

hours_note: Most mechanical engineers work full time, and some work more than 40 hours a week.
notes: Solid upside with broad industry relevance, but it demands stronger technical preparation than many students realize.

hours_note: Most social workers are employed full time. They sometimes work evenings, weekends, and holidays to see clients or attend meetings, and they may be on call.
notes: Impactful and widely needed, but the emotional load is real and premium earnings often require clinical specialization or leadership progression.

hours_note: Most paralegals and legal assistants work full time.
notes: A practical legal-services route with predictable office hours for many roles, but document-heavy work is exposed to AI and many higher-paying jobs cluster near bigger metros.

hours_note: Many dental hygienists work part time, and some work for more than one dentist.
notes: One of the strongest shorter healthcare credentials for earnings and schedule quality.

hours_note: Most radiologic and MRI technologists work full time. Some work evenings, weekends, or overnight.
notes: Good technical-healthcare path with moderate pay, but hospitals may require off-hour coverage.

hours_note: Respiratory therapists may work nights, weekends, and holidays because medical facilities are always open.
notes: Strong licensed healthcare route with good demand, but hospital-based roles can mean night and weekend coverage.

hours_note: Carpenters usually work full time and may work evenings or weekends to meet deadlines.
notes: Durable local work with tangible output and a clearer end-of-day boundary than many office careers, though the job is physically demanding and weather-sensitive.

hours_note: Welders, cutters, solderers, and brazers usually work full time, and some work shifts.
notes: Fast route into skilled industrial work with lower debt and lower AI pressure than many office jobs, but the work is physically intense and some plants run off-hours.
